image: epithelioid cell type and prominent nucleoli (arrow).jpg
res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense infiltrate of small to medium-sized cells with round to oval nuclei, finely stippled chromatin, and inconspicuous nucleoli. The cells are arranged in a somewhat cohesive manner, forming clusters and nests within a relatively acellular background. There is no significant evidence of necrosis, mitotic figures, or significant cellular pleomorphism.

Key supporting features: Small round cells; nested architecture; inconspicuous nucleoli; acellular background.

Likely interpretation: Melanoma (intraocular); differential includes lymphoma, metastatic carcinoma, or other small round cell tumors.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: epithelioid cell type and prominent nucleoli is shown. Epithelioid melanoma cells with prominent nucleoli are present.

image: low power shows a tumor in the subretinal area.jpg
res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a section of corneal tissue with a central, well-circumscribed, and homogenous mass. The mass appears to be composed of a uniform population of spindle-shaped cells with elongated nuclei and minimal cytoplasm. There is no significant cellular pleomorphism or atypia noted. The interface with the surrounding stroma is smooth, without evidence of invasion or infiltration. The overlying epithelium appears intact without signs of dysplasia or ulceration.

Key supporting features: spindle cells; uniform nuclei; smooth interface; intact epithelium

Likely interpretation: Benign corneal spindle cell lesion; differential includes fibrous histiocytoma, dermatofibroma, or other benign spindle cell proliferations.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: low power tumor in the subretinal area is shown. Low power view shows an intraocular melanoma mass involving the posterior choroid/subretinal region.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cellular proliferation with a predominantly epithelioid cell population. The cells exhibit moderate nuclear enlargement, hyperchromasia, and occasional prominent nucleoli. There is a suggestion of a nested growth pattern with some areas showing a more solid arrangement. The stroma appears relatively hypocellular, with minimal desmoplastic reaction.

Key supporting features: epithelioid cells; nuclear enlargement; hyperchromasia; prominent nucleoli; nested growth pattern

Likely interpretation: Malignant melanoma; spindle cell carcinoma; metastatic adenocarcinoma (differential)
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: mitotic figure is shown. Mitotic activity is identified in the melanoma cells.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cluster of small, round to oval cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and minimal cytoplasm. There is a focal area where the cells appear more cohesive, forming a slightly nodular structure. The background shows a mix of similar cells and some scattered larger, more pleomorphic cells with prominent nucleoli. There is no obvious interface with adjacent tissue, and the cells do not show significant surface involvement or desmoplastic reaction.

Key supporting features: hyperchromatic nuclei; small round cells; focal nodular growth; scattered pleomorphic cells

Likely interpretation: Small cell carcinoma (undifferentiated) or poorly differentiated carcinoma; differential includes lymphoma, metastatic small cell carcinoma, or other undifferentiated carcinomas.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: mitotic figure is shown. Mitotic activity is identified in the melanoma cells.

image: spindle A cell type has nuclear grooving (arrow).jpg
res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cellular proliferation with a predominantly epithelioid cell population. The cells exhibit moderate nuclear enlargement, hyperchromasia, and occasional prominent nucleoli. There is a focal area with more pleomorphic nuclei and increased mitotic figures. The stroma appears relatively hypocellular with scattered collagenous fibers.

Key supporting features: epithelioid; hyperchromatic nuclei; mitotic figures; focal pleomorphism

Likely interpretation: Malignant melanoma; differential includes metastatic carcinoma, sarcoma, or other epithelial malignancies.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: spindle A cell type with nuclear grooving is shown. Spindle A melanoma cells with nuclear grooves are present.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cellular proliferation with a predominantly spindle-shaped cell population. The nuclei are variably sized, some showing hyperchromasia and occasional pleomorphism. There is a moderate amount of cytoplasm, which appears eosinophilic. The architecture is somewhat disorganized, with areas of overlapping and interdigitating cells. There is no obvious evidence of significant necrosis or mitotic activity.

Key supporting features: spindle cells; hyperchromatic nuclei; eosinophilic cytoplasm; disorganized architecture

Likely interpretation: Intraocular spindle cell neoplasm (e.g., melanoma, leiomyosarcoma); further evaluation required for definitive diagnosis.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: spindle B cell type is shown. Spindle B melanoma cells are present.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense infiltrate of atypical cells with pleomorphic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and scattered mitotic figures. The cells are arranged in a sheet-like pattern without distinct glandular or architectural organization. There is no evidence of surface involvement or epithelial dysplasia. The stroma appears relatively unremarkable, with no significant desmoplasia or inflammation.

Key supporting features: Atypical cells; pleomorphic nuclei; prominent nucleoli; scattered mitotic figures; sheet-like arrangement

Likely interpretation: Atypical intraocular lymphoid proliferation; differential includes lymphoma, reactive lymphoid hyperplasia, or other lymphoproliferative disorders.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: spindle cell type is shown. Spindle melanoma cells are present.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cellular proliferation with a predominantly spindle-shaped cell population. The nuclei are variably sized, some showing mild hyperchromasia and occasional nuclear molding. There is a moderate amount of cytoplasm, which appears eosinophilic. The architecture is somewhat disorganized, with areas of overlapping cells and minimal stromal matrix. There is no overt evidence of necrosis or significant mitotic activity.

Key supporting features: spindle cells; mild nuclear pleomorphism; eosinophilic cytoplasm; disorganized architecture

Likely interpretation: Intraocular spindle cell neoplasm; differential includes schwannoma, leiomyoma, or fibrous histiocytoma (with low-grade malignancy).
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: spindle B cell type is shown. Spindle B melanoma cells are present.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense, infiltrative growth of spindle-shaped cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and minimal cytoplasm. There is a significant stromal reaction with fibrosis and scattered areas of necrosis. The interface with the underlying tissue appears infiltrative, suggesting a possible aggressive behavior.

Key supporting features: Infiltrative growth; hyperchromatic nuclei; fibrosis; necrosis

Likely interpretation: Malignant spindle cell neoplasm (e.g., leiomyosarcoma, fibrosarcoma); further evaluation required for definitive diagnosis.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: tumor invades sclera is shown. Melanoma invades the sclera.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense, infiltrative growth of spindle-shaped cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and minimal cytoplasm. There is a prominent desmoplastic stroma with scattered areas of necrosis. The interface with the underlying tissue appears infiltrative, with no obvious pushing border.

Key supporting features: Desmoplastic stroma; hyperchromatic nuclei; necrosis; infiltrative growth pattern.

Likely interpretation: Intraocular spindle cell neoplasm (e.g., melanoma, leiomyosarcoma); further evaluation required for definitive diagnosis.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: tumor invades sclera is shown. Melanoma invades the sclera.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense, infiltrative lesion involving the corneal stroma, extending into the anterior sclera. The stroma appears edematous with a mixed inflammatory infiltrate, including lymphocytes and plasma cells. There is a prominent interface with the underlying episcleral tissue, suggesting a possible infiltrative growth pattern. The lesion has a pushing border and appears to be invading the sclera.

Key supporting features: Infiltrative; edematous stroma; mixed inflammatory infiltrate; pushing border; scleral invasion.

Likely interpretation: Intraocular tumor (e.g., uveal melanoma); differential includes lymphoma, metastatic carcinoma, or other infiltrative neoplasms.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: tumor invades sclera is shown. Melanoma invades the sclera.

res:
Microscopic description: The image shows a dense cluster of large, pleomorphic cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and prominent nucleoli, set against a background of erythrocytes and other cellular debris. The cells appear to be infiltrating the surrounding tissue, which includes a mix of connective tissue and blood vessels.

Key supporting features: Pleomorphic nuclei; hyperchromasia; prominent nucleoli; infiltrative growth; erythrocyte admixture.

Likely interpretation: Infiltrative neoplasm; differential includes lymphoma, metastatic carcinoma, or sarcoma.
gts:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: vitreous seeding of tumor cells is shown. Pigmented melanoma cells are present in the vitreous, consistent with vitreous seeding.
